WinterWheat
- Crop type: cereal grain crop (non-legume, C3).
- Nitrogen role: does not fix nitrogen and generally relies on available soil nitrogen.
- Rooting and water: deep-rooted (max root depth about 2.0 m) with moderate water demand.
- Temperature and maturity: cooler-adapted than corn or soybean (optimum development temperature about 20 C), cold-tolerant, and longer-season than SpringWheat (maturity thermal time about 1800).
- Rotation role: a cool-season cereal option that diversifies rotations but does not provide the nitrogen-building benefit of soybean.
